title: crocus reticulatus subvar. rectilimbus
intro: Crocus reticulatus subvar. rectilimbus, also known as the netted corm, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Iridaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and a laxative, which have been used to treat conditions such as kidney stones and constipation. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including urinary tract problems, and its extracts have been found to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of Crocus reticulatus subvar. rectilimbus include dried corms, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal potential.
